subtitle: SBOM, open-source notices and security reports for a single project — in one run

form.title: Scan settings
form.description: Enter your project details and pick what to generate.

source.label: Scan target
source.currentDirHint: Scans the source in this folder (mounted as /src). To scan a different folder, run --ui from there.
source.rootfsHint: Scans an OS rootfs or any subfolder under the current folder (/src) as a directory. Paths outside the folder are rejected — place the rootfs under the launch folder, mount it with --mount, or use a Docker image instead.
source.rootfsSubpathHint: Leave empty to scan the whole mounted folder, or narrow the scan to a subfolder inside it.
source.removeScanFolder: Remove this folder from the list
source.scanFolderDesktopHint: Added folders are read-only scan targets. Adding or removing one restarts the scanner briefly.
source.gitHint: Public repos clone directly; for private repos enter a token below.
source.gitTokenHint: Only for private repos. Used once for the clone; never stored or logged.
source.sbomAnalyzeHint: Notice + security reports are enabled automatically for the risk analysis.
source.firmwareHint: Unpacks, then identifies components with syft + cve-bin-tool (.bin/.img/.squashfs…).
source.firmwareUnavailable: Firmware analysis needs Docker running (the firmware image runs as a sibling container).
source.siblingPullNotice: First run pulls the dedicated image (a large, one-time download); later runs reuse it.
source.dockerHint: Scans a local/remote Docker image (Docker socket required).
source.aiModelHint: Enter a HuggingFace model ID as 'org/model' (e.g. Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B) — not a collection name or a full URL. Fetches model-card metadata over the network to build a CycloneDX ML-BOM.
source.aiModelUnavailable: AI-model SBOMs need Docker running (the AIBOM image runs as a sibling container).

options.aiNoticeHint: For an AI model the notice lists the model and dataset licenses and flags use-restricted ones (RAIL, non-commercial) for review — worth keeping on.
options.spdxHint: Also export the SBOM as SPDX 2.3 JSON, converted from the CycloneDX output
options.deepLicenseHint: Scans your own source files with ScanCode for per-file license text and headers, in addition to the licenses each package declares (slow).
options.scanMethodHint: Advanced analysis for source scanned without a package manager. If your project builds with one (Maven, npm, pip, Go modules…), the SBOM is already complete and these can stay off.
options.identifyVendoredHint: Finds third-party open source copied into the source tree (mainly C/C++) with SCANOSS. Only file fingerprints — never the source — go to the OSSKB service.
options.scanossTokenHint: The free OSSKB endpoint is rate-limited — fine for an occasional scan, but frequent runs may return nothing. For regular use, add a token from scanoss.com. Used once for this scan; never stored or logged.
options.includeOsvHint: Adds OSV.dev advisories to the firmware vulnerability match. The OSV database is not bundled in the image, so it downloads from osv.dev on this run (progress is shown below).
options.byteStableHint: Produces a byte-for-byte identical SBOM when the same input is re-scanned, so outputs can be diffed and checksummed. Skips network license lookups, which can otherwise vary between runs.
options.uploadHint: Send the generated SBOM to a Dependency-Track or TRUSCA server after the scan. The server URL and token are used for this run only and never stored.

upload.tokenHint: Dependency-Track uses an X-Api-Key; TRUSCA uses a Bearer token. The token is used for this run only and never stored.

progress.title: Run log
progress.waiting: Live logs will appear here once a scan starts.

result.title: Results
result.rescanHint: Scan the same target again with these settings prefilled
result.aiScanBody: Generated from HuggingFace model metadata. It centers on the model and its datasets, so it has fewer components than a typical software scan.
result.vendoredHintTitle: Few components found — is this C/C++ embedded source?
result.vendoredHintBody: Open source is often copied (vendored) into C/C++ sources that have no package manager, where a normal scan can't see it. Turn on "Detect copied-in open source" under Advanced scan options and run again. Only file fingerprints are sent — your source stays local.
result.sbomDegradedTitle: Direct dependencies only — deep resolution didn't run
result.sbomDegradedBody: The transitive-dependency analyzer (cdxgen) couldn't run, so this SBOM lists only direct dependencies. Make sure Docker is available and re-scan for the full dependency graph.
result.sbomDegradedDisk: The transitive-dependency analyzer (cdxgen) ran out of Docker disk space, so this SBOM lists only direct dependencies. Free space (e.g. "docker system prune") and re-scan for the full dependency graph.
result.scanossUnavailableBody: The OSSKB lookup was blocked (rate limit, no network, or no token). The free OSSKB endpoint is rate-limited for anonymous use — add a SCANOSS token under Advanced scan options and run again to get results.
result.scanossNoMatchBody: SCANOSS searched successfully but found no third-party open source copied directly into the source — either there is none, or the code isn't in OSSKB.
result.vendoredBadgeHint: Identified by SCANOSS as open source copied into the sources
result.securityScanFailed: Security scan failed — no results were produced. The counts here do not reflect the target's actual exposure.
result.securityScanPartial: The security scanner reported an error; these results may be incomplete.

riskReport.title: Open-source risk report
riskReport.desc: Consolidated supply-chain risk analysis across licenses and vulnerabilities.

sbom.title: SBOM (CycloneDX)
sbom.desc: The normalized software bill of materials in CycloneDX JSON, plus an SPDX 2.3 conversion when enabled.

notice.title: Open-source notice
notice.desc: License notices and attributions for the bundled components.

conformance.title: Conformance report
conformance.desc: Format validation of the submitted SBOM.

security.title: Security report
security.desc: Known vulnerabilities (CVE) found in the components.

signature.title: SBOM signature
signature.desc: Cosign signature for verifying the SBOM.

result.eolBadgeHint: Past upstream end-of-life — no security patches expected
result.outdatedBadgeHint: A newer patch is available in its release cycle

deps.loadError: Could not load the SBOM for the dependency view.
deps.flatFallback: This SBOM has no dependency graph; showing a flat component list.
deps.tooLarge: The graph has {{count}} nodes (over {{cap}}); use the tree view instead.
deps.graphError: The dependency graph could not be rendered.
deps.arrowHint: Arrows point from a package to what it depends on.
deps.interactHint: Hover to trace a package's links; click for details.
deps.keyboardHint: Keyboard users: switch to Tree to navigate.
deps.graphAria: Dependency graph (visual). Switch to Tree view to navigate by keyboard.

sourceTree.loadError: Could not load the source file tree.
sourceTree.noLicenseHint: License details appear when you run a ScanCode (deep-license) scan.

nav.label: Sections
nav.sectionsHint: Run a scan to see result sections here.

newscan.title: What do you want to scan?
newscan.subtitle: Pick a target and BomLens generates the SBOM, open-source notice, and security report locally in one run.

run.failedBody: Something went wrong before the scan could complete. Check the run log below for details, then try again.

models.loadError: Could not load the model SBOM.
models.disclosureHint: Whether each axis is documented in the model card — not a claim about the model itself.

g7.subtitle: G7 minimum elements for AI
g7.allAdvisory: All G7 elements are advisory (a non-binding guideline).

g7-meta-author.what: Who created the SBOM (author, supplier or manufacturer).

g7-meta-version.what: The version of this SBOM document.

g7-meta-format-name.what: The SBOM data format name (e.g. CycloneDX).

g7-meta-signature.what: A cryptographic signature from the SBOM author.
g7-meta-signature.fix: Sign the SBOM and add the signature block.

g7-meta-tool-name.what: The tool that generated the SBOM.
g7-meta-tool-name.fix: Record the generating tool under metadata.tools.

g7-meta-tool-version.what: The version of the generating tool.
g7-meta-tool-version.fix: Record the tool version under metadata.tools.

g7-meta-gen-context.what: The context the SBOM was generated in (build or analysis).

g7-slp-name.what: The name of the overall system.
g7-slp-name.fix: Set metadata.component.name on the (non-model) root component.

g7-slp-components.what: The components that make up the system.
g7-slp-components.fix: List the system's components in the SBOM.

g7-slp-data-flow.what: How data flows through the system.
g7-slp-data-flow.fix: Document the system data flow — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-slp-data-usage.fix: Document the system's data usage — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-slp-io.what: The system's input and output properties.
g7-slp-io.fix: Document the system inputs and outputs — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-slp-app-area.what: The intended application area of the system.
g7-slp-app-area.fix: Document the intended application area — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-model-name.fix: Set name on the machine-learning-model component.

g7-model-id.what: Identifies the model with a standard identifier such as PURL/CPE.
g7-model-id.fix: Add a purl or cpe to the model component in the SBOM.

g7-model-version.fix: Set version on the model component.

g7-model-timestamp.fix: Add a created/timestamp property to the model component.

g7-model-producer.fix: Set publisher, supplier or manufacturer on the model component.

g7-model-description.fix: Set description on the model component.

g7-model-hash-value.what: Integrity hashes for the model weight files.
g7-model-hash-value.fix: Add the model file hashes (e.g. SHA-256) to the component's hashes.

g7-model-hash-alg.what: The hash algorithm used for the model files.
g7-model-hash-alg.fix: Set alg (e.g. SHA-256) on each entry in the component's hashes.

g7-model-card.what: Model-card info such as architecture and training parameters.

g7-model-io.what: The model's input and output properties.

g7-model-training.what: Training properties such as datasets and architecture.

g7-model-license.what: The license that applies to the model.
g7-model-license.fix: State a license on the model card, or fill the licenses field on the SBOM component.

g7-model-openness.what: How open the weights, architecture, data and training are.
g7-model-openness.fix: Declare the model's openness (public weights/data/training code) via openness:* properties.

g7-model-extref.what: External references for the model (distribution, docs).
g7-model-extref.fix: Add externalReferences to the model component.

g7-ds-name.what: The name of each training dataset.
g7-ds-name.fix: Add data components (or model-card datasets) with a name.

g7-ds-description.fix: Set description on the data component.

g7-ds-content.fix: Document the dataset content — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-ds-identifier.what: A stable identifier for the dataset.
g7-ds-identifier.fix: Set bom-ref or purl on the data component.

g7-ds-hash.fix: Add dataset file hashes — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-ds-provenance.fix: Set componentData.governance or a provenance/source property.

g7-ds-statistics.fix: Document dataset statistics — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-ds-sensitivity.what: Sensitive content in the dataset (PII or copyright).
g7-ds-sensitivity.fix: Document dataset sensitivity — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-ds-dependency.fix: Document dataset dependencies — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-ds-license.fix: Add licenses to the data component.

g7-infra-software.what: The software dependencies of the infrastructure.
g7-infra-software.fix: Include the library/application/framework components in the SBOM.

g7-infra-hardware.what: The hardware the system runs on (HBOM link).
g7-infra-hardware.fix: Add device/hardware components or a hardware BOM reference.

g7-sec-controls.what: The security controls applied to the system.
g7-sec-controls.fix: Document the security controls — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-sec-compliance.what: The security compliance the system meets.
g7-sec-compliance.fix: Document security compliance — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-sec-policy.fix: Document the cybersecurity policy — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-sec-vulns.what: Known vulnerabilities referenced for the system.
g7-sec-vulns.fix: Add a vulnerabilities section (VEX or advisories) to the SBOM.

g7-kpi-security.fix: Document security metrics — no automated source, human review needed.

g7-kpi-operational.what: Operational performance KPIs of the model.

crosswalk.title: Regulatory crosswalk
crosswalk.disclaimer: A documentation-preparation aid, not a compliance verdict. BomLens does not certify regulatory compliance; it only shows which documentation obligation each G7 element touches.
crosswalk.totals: {{present}} present, {{gap}} gap, {{review}} need review of {{total}} mapped

aiProfile.title: AI compliance profile
aiProfile.note: A one-glance rollup of the AI SBOM findings for documentation review — it makes no compliance determination.

licenses.clickHint: Click a license to see its components

recent.title: Scan management
recent.subtitle: Scans saved in this machine's local output folder.
recent.emptyBody: Scan a folder, Git repo, Docker image, SBOM or AI model. BomLens builds the bill of materials, license notice and security report locally, in one run.
recent.source: Delete a file and it leaves this list too.

validation.required: Enter a project name and version (and any required input).
